# Report data summary — auto-generated

Generated by `scripts/generate_report.py` from the aggregated CSVs in
`results/logs_personal/aggregated/`. Tables under `report/generated/tables/`
are paste-ready (Markdown) or `\input{}`-ready (LaTeX).

## W1 — Multi-seed variance and paired tests

Each row in `agg_pruning_matrix.csv` aggregates 3 seeds and reports mean ± std for
balanced accuracy, melanoma sensitivity, AUROC, ECE, and DCR. Paired t-tests
between Wanda / Taylor / random and the magnitude baseline live in
`paired_tests_pruning_matrix.csv`. Headline numbers below.

**Wanda vs. magnitude at 50% sparsity (paired t-test, n=3)**

| Model | Metric | mean diff (mag − wanda) | p | sig? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| deit_small | balanced_acc | +0.1816 | 0.007 | ✓ |
| deit_small | mel_sensitivity | +0.0184 | 0.463 |   |
| deit_small | bcc_sensitivity | +0.3806 | 0.002 | ✓ |
| deit_small | akiec_sensitivity | -0.1333 | 0.125 |   |
| deit_tiny | balanced_acc | +0.3843 | 0.002 | ✓ |
| deit_tiny | mel_sensitivity | -0.3780 | 0.000 | ✓ |
| deit_tiny | bcc_sensitivity | +0.2851 | 0.000 | ✓ |
| deit_tiny | akiec_sensitivity | +0.5563 | 0.005 | ✓ |

Per-cell mean ± std for every (criterion, sparsity) is in `tables/pruning_headline.{md,tex,csv}`.

## W2 — Lesion-grouped split (data leakage fix)

All experiments now use `GroupShuffleSplit` on `lesion_id` (see `data/dataset.py:get_train_val_splits`).
Code asserts no overlap; any image of a given lesion goes entirely to train OR val.
Re-run all training/eval used the corrected split. No prior-image-level results survive in this report.

## W3 — Medical evaluation metrics

Every pruning row reports: per-class AUROC (macro + melanoma one-vs-rest),
specificity at 90% sensitivity, sensitivity at 90% specificity, top-label ECE,
per-class precision (PPV), and the dangerous-class degradation ratio (DCR).

See the `Mel AUROC`, `Macro AUROC`, `ECE`, `Spec@90Sens`, and `DCR` columns in
`tables/pruning_headline.md` (paste into the medical-metrics table of the paper).

## W4 — Wanda failure mechanism

Per-layer activation statistics (kurtosis, top-5% concentration, outlier ratio) and
their correlation with single-layer Wanda damage are in
`results/logs_personal/seed_0/activation_stats_correlation.csv`.

**Correlations (seed 0, deit_small):**

| Statistic | Pearson r | Spearman ρ | n layers | criterion |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| kurtosis | 0.021 | 0.105 | 48 | wanda |
| top5pct_concentration | -0.029 | 0.035 | 48 | wanda |
| outlier_ratio | 0.004 | -0.027 | 48 | wanda |
| kurtosis | -0.070 | 0.033 | 48 | magnitude |
| top5pct_concentration | 0.033 | 0.090 | 48 | magnitude |
| outlier_ratio | -0.033 | -0.033 | 48 | magnitude |

A positive Pearson/Spearman value with the wanda criterion means: layers with more
concentrated / heavy-tailed activations are the ones Wanda damages most. That is the
evidence the paper's hypothesis needs.

## W5 — Second dataset replication

**Not run.** The personal-compute config explicitly drops the second-dataset arm.
Reframe the paper claim to *HAM10000 dermoscopy* throughout — `report/related_work.md`
already structures this. The harness exists at `experiments/e_second_dataset.py` for future work.

## W6 — Paxton + X-Pruner + SparseGPT-pseudo baselines

All three baselines ran on 2 models × 3 criteria × 3 sparsities.
See `tables/baselines.md`. Compare to the matching rows in `tables/pruning_headline.md`.

## W7 — CNN baseline (MobileNetV2)

MobileNetV2 ran with the full criterion × sparsity sweep + recovery fine-tuning.
See `agg_mobilenet_results.csv`. **Reframe in the paper: this is an exploratory check,
not a generalisation argument** (depthwise convs behave atypically under unstructured pruning;
ResNet-50 was dropped from the personal-compute budget).

## W8 — Honest size columns

Every CSV now reports three sizes: `dense_size_kb` (parameter count × 4),
`disk_size_kb` (real torch.save bytes), and `effective_sparse_size_kb`
(nonzero params × 4, theoretical sparse-storage ceiling). Use `disk_size_kb` for
honest deployment numbers; flag `effective_sparse_size_kb` as a ceiling under sparse storage.
2:4 structured sparsity was not run on Apple Silicon (no Ampere sparse cores).

## W9 — Edge latency

Latency benchmarked on the run host (Apple M-series MacBook) against 2 runtimes:
`torch_cpu`, `torch_mps`.
See `tables/edge_latency.md`. The `mean_ms` and `p95_ms` columns are the headline numbers.

## W10 — Recovery sweep

Recovery sweep covers epochs ∈ [np.int64(5), np.int64(10), np.int64(20)] × sparsities ∈ [np.float64(0.5)].
See `tables/recovery.md`. To answer 'does Wanda catch up with more recovery?',
compare each criterion's `Balanced Acc` and `Mel Sens` columns at 5 vs 20 recovery epochs.

## W11 — Non-uniform allocation policies

Policies compared: ['binned_default', 'binned_k3', 'continuous_t1', 'dense', 'uniform']. See `tables/nonuniform.md`.

## W12 — Attention overlap (quantitative)

Skipped — no ISIC 2018 segmentation masks staged at `data/ham10000/segmentation_masks/`.
Qualitative figure in `results/figures_personal/fig8_attention_maps.pdf` is still produced.
Stage masks and rerun pillar 6 if you want IoU + pointing-game numbers.

## W13 — Dangerous-class degradation ratio

DCR = (mean drop on mel/akiec/bcc) / (mean drop on nv/bkl/df/vasc).
Reported in every pruning, recovery, baseline, non-uniform, and CNN table.
A value > 1.0 means compression preferentially forgets dangerous classes.

## W14 — Code release

Repo pushed to `emhmad/TinyML_Project_2`, commit `caeda30`. Includes `environment.yml`,
`scripts/precompute_masks.py`, `scripts/release.py`, `scripts/reproduce.sh`, configs for both
cluster (`multi_seed_ciai_fast.yaml`) and personal (`local_personal_8h.yaml`) reproduction.

## W15 — Figures

Re-rendered Fig 7 (recovery comparison, wide canvas, error bars) and Fig 9
(calibration ablation, log₂ ticks, reference band) under `results/figures_personal/`.
See the regeneration commands at the end of this file.

## W16 — Clinical deployability

Each aggregated table has a `*_clinical.csv` companion with a `clinical_regime` column
({triage_screen / specialist_referral / primary_diagnosis / academic_only}). Thresholds
live in `evaluation/clinical_thresholds.py` (`DEFAULT_THRESHOLDS`). Source citations needed
in the paper Discussion — the module's docstring lists the rough anchors used.

## W17 — Related work

Structured citation skeleton at `report/related_work.md`. Promote into the paper's Related
Work section, expanding each block with full prose.

## How to regenerate this report

```bash
python -m scripts.generate_report --root results/logs_personal
python -m plotting.fig7_recovery --config configs/local_personal_8h.yaml
python -m plotting.fig9_calibration_ablation --config configs/local_personal_8h.yaml
```
